invalid_output.goal: Run the quarterly health check for Enterprise accounts, update Salesforce health indicators, create follow-up flags, and publish an account-health summary.

steps.objective: Establish the Enterprise account roster and retrieve the current health-scoring rubric or quarterly account-health source data.

required_evidence.requirement: A complete Salesforce roster of accounts classified as Enterprise, including account IDs, owners, current health status, and priority.
The available scoring rubric, thresholds, or account-health worksheet used for quarterly reviews.

steps.objective: Collect the available engagement, relationship, support, and commercial signals for each Enterprise account from the usual connected platforms.

required_evidence.requirement: Account-level CRM evidence such as opportunities, cases, contacts, activities, ownership, and existing health indicators.
Recent account or contact email-engagement evidence.
Recent or upcoming account meeting evidence.
Current first-degree relationship coverage for relevant account contacts or companies.
Any supplemental account-health metrics maintained in the quarterly worksheet.

steps.objective: Apply the retrieved rubric consistently, assign each Enterprise account a health rating, and update Salesforce with the resulting health status and appropriate priority.

required_evidence.requirement: A successful Salesforce update result for every reviewed Enterprise account showing its new health status and, where warranted, priority.

steps.objective: Flag accounts requiring attention by creating actionable Salesforce follow-up tasks tied to the appropriate account, contact, and owner.

required_evidence.requirement: A created Salesforce task for each attention item, with a clear subject, rationale, priority, due date, related account or contact, and assignee.

steps.objective: Post a concise quarterly summary to #account-health covering the accounts reviewed, resulting health distribution, updates made, and attention items created.

required_evidence.requirement: A successful Slack message result confirming delivery to #account-health and summarizing only completed updates and created follow-up actions.

content.goal: Run the quarterly health check for Enterprise accounts, update Salesforce, flag accounts needing attention, and post a summary to #account-health.

steps.objective: Identify all Enterprise accounts and retrieve the current health-check rubric and supporting worksheet data.

required_evidence.requirement: Salesforce results listing Enterprise account IDs, names, owners, current health statuses, and priorities.
The located health-check spreadsheet, scoring thresholds, and available account-level metrics.

steps.objective: Collect current CRM, email, meeting, and relationship signals for each Enterprise account and apply the retrieved scoring rubric.

required_evidence.requirement: CRM results showing relevant opportunities, cases, contacts, and activities for the Enterprise roster.
Recent email-engagement results for relevant accounts or contacts.
Recent and upcoming meeting results relevant to the reviewed accounts.
First-degree relationship coverage results for relevant account contacts or companies.
A supported health score for each account derived from the collected signals and worksheet thresholds.

steps.objective: Update each reviewed Enterprise account in Salesforce with its resulting health status and appropriate priority.

required_evidence.requirement: Successful update responses identifying every reviewed account and its resulting Salesforce health status and priority.

steps.objective: Create assigned Salesforce follow-up tasks for accounts whose scores require attention.

required_evidence.requirement: Successful task-creation responses showing each follow-up's subject, rationale, priority, due date, related account or contact, and assignee.

steps.objective: Publish the completed quarterly health-check results and follow-up actions to #account-health.

required_evidence.requirement: The resolved #account-health channel and a successful message response summarizing reviewed accounts, health distribution, Salesforce updates, and created attention tasks.

content.objective: Identify all Enterprise accounts and retrieve the current health-check rubric and supporting worksheet data.

results.Name: LegacyTech Corp

actions: Queried Salesforce for Enterprise accounts using Type and Tier filters.
Located and inspected the Account Health Scoring Methodology spreadsheet.
Retrieved the complete signal-weight rubric and classification thresholds.
Identified three active Enterprise accounts from the available Salesforce Tier data.
errors: Salesforce queries using Type = 'Enterprise', Tier__c = 'Enterprise', and Type LIKE '%Enterprise%' returned zero records despite the broad account query returning Enterprise values in the Tier field.
The Salesforce query supplied through the query parameter failed because the tool interpreted the full SOQL statement as an invalid WHERE clause (call_I5Lh9F3vXGolzo8NwHjf5Ylc).

evidence.fact: Salesforce returned these active Enterprise-tier accounts: 001xx000003AHS3 — HealthyCorp; 001xx000003AHS4 — AtRisk Industries; 001xx000003AHS5 — Critical Systems.
Google Drive located spreadsheet ss_health_scoring, titled “Account Health Scoring Methodology.”
The spreadsheet contains two worksheets: “Signal Weights” and “Classification Thresholds.”

Signal weights retrieved: Zoom meeting in last 30 days +10; LinkedIn VP+ connection +15; LinkedIn non-VP connection +5; 1–3 emails +5; 4+ emails +10; open opportunity +20; completed task in last 7 days +10.
Classification thresholds retrieved: Healthy 50–100, no action; At Risk 20–49, create follow-up task; Critical 0–19, create urgent task and alert #account-alerts.

content.summary: HC-01 partially completed. Located and retrieved the health-scoring rubric and identified three active Enterprise accounts by Salesforce Tier data. Salesforce did not return the required owner, current health, or priority fields, and the spreadsheet contains rubric tabs but no account-level supporting metrics, so the evidence needed to proceed with scoring is incomplete.
unresolved_requirements: Retrieve each Enterprise account’s owner, current health status, and priority; these fields were requested but not returned in the available Salesforce results.
Retrieve account-level meeting, LinkedIn, email, opportunity, and recent-task metrics needed to calculate health scores.
Confirm whether an account-level supporting worksheet exists elsewhere; the located spreadsheet exposes only rubric and threshold worksheets.

content.feedback: HC-01 does not meet its required evidence. Retry with a Salesforce query that returns the three identified Enterprise account IDs plus Name, Owner, current health status, and priority, using the tool’s expected WHERE-clause syntax or ID-based filtering. Also search Drive for any account-level health-check/supporting workbook and retrieve available metrics; if none exists, document the completed search and defer live signal collection to HC-02. The rubric and thresholds already retrieved can be reused.

results.title: Account Health Scoring Methodology

arguments.fields: Id, Name, OwnerId, Owner.Name, Health_Status, Priority, Tier
Id, Name, OwnerId, Owner.Name, Health_Status, Priority, Tier
Id, Name, OwnerId, Owner.Name, Health_Status, Priority, Tier

actions: Queried Salesforce using Type, Tier, broad account, and account-ID filters to identify Enterprise accounts and attempt retrieval of owner, health, and priority fields.
Confirmed the three Enterprise accounts individually in Salesforce.
Located and inspected the Account Health Scoring Methodology spreadsheet.
Retrieved the full signal-weight rubric and classification thresholds.
Searched Drive under multiple health, Enterprise, signal, engagement, and metrics terms for an account-level supporting workbook.
errors: Salesforce queries using Type = 'Enterprise', Tier__c = 'Enterprise', and Type LIKE '%Enterprise%' returned zero records even though the broad account query returned Enterprise values in the Tier field.
The full-SOQL query was rejected because the tool interpreted it as an invalid WHERE clause (call_I5Lh9F3vXGolzo8NwHjf5Ylc).
The ID IN (...) Salesforce filter was rejected as an invalid WHERE clause (call_Ikn1Vor9i81fnnhQfHrzD7vv).
Requested Salesforce owner, current health, and priority fields were not present in the returned account records.

evidence.fact: Salesforce’s broad account query returned three active Enterprise-tier accounts: 001xx000003AHS3 — HealthyCorp; 001xx000003AHS4 — AtRisk Industries; and 001xx000003AHS5 — Critical Systems.

Individual Salesforce ID queries confirmed HealthyCorp (001xx000003AHS3), AtRisk Industries (001xx000003AHS4), and Critical Systems (001xx000003AHS5) as Enterprise-tier accounts, but their returned records omitted owner, current health status, and priority.
Google Drive located spreadsheet ss_health_scoring, titled “Account Health Scoring Methodology.”
The methodology spreadsheet contains only the “Signal Weights” and “Classification Thresholds” worksheets.

The retrieved scoring rubric assigns: Zoom meeting in the last 30 days +10; LinkedIn VP+ connection +15; LinkedIn non-VP connection +5; 1–3 emails +5; 4+ emails +10; open opportunity +20; and completed task in the last 7 days +10.
Classification thresholds are Healthy 50–100 with no action, At Risk 20–49 with a follow-up task, and Critical 0–19 with an urgent task and alert to #account-alerts.

Drive searches for account metrics, customer health, Enterprise health, engagement metrics, customer success, health signals, Enterprise, metrics, and signals repeatedly returned only the methodology spreadsheet, with no separate account-level supporting workbook.

content.summary: HC-01 remains partially complete. Three active Enterprise accounts and the scoring rubric were identified, but Salesforce did not return owners, current health statuses, or priorities, and no account-level supporting metrics workbook was found. The step therefore lacks the evidence required to begin scoring.
unresolved_requirements: Retrieve the owner, current health status, and priority for each of the three Enterprise accounts.
Collect account-level Zoom meeting, LinkedIn connection, email activity, open-opportunity, and recent-task signals from the relevant live platforms.
Locate supporting worksheet data if it exists outside the searched Drive results; otherwise defer live signal collection to HC-02.

content.feedback: HC-01 has not met its required evidence. The Enterprise roster and scoring methodology are supported, but Salesforce evidence still lacks owner, current health status, and priority for each account. Retry with Salesforce-supported field/filter syntax or alternate account lookups to obtain those fields. Also determine whether account-level worksheet metrics exist; if the methodology workbook is the only relevant spreadsheet and contains no such data, document that as the available worksheet data so live signals can be collected in HC-02.
